items.headline: U.S. Futures Decline Amid Geopolitical Tensions; Oil Prices Rise
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are trading lower this Thursday, indicating a potential reversal of Wednesday's gains, as renewed uncertainty surrounding the U.S.-Iran conflict weighs on investor sentiment. Oil prices have moved higher, contributing to concerns over inflation and global supply chains. Asian and European markets also opened lower.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Inc. Rises
items.summary: Shares of Super Micro Computer Inc. are showing early gains.

items.headline: SK Hynix Shares Decline
items.summary: SK Hynix, a key memory chip maker for AI, saw its shares drop amidst news of a Google breakthrough impacting memory demand.

items.headline: Micron Technology Falls
items.summary: Micron Technology shares continued their decline after hours, following a 3.4% loss on Wednesday due to the same Google AI memory news.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates Concerns, Pushing Oil Higher
items.summary: Hopes for a ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran conflict have diminished today as Tehran reportedly rejected peace proposals, leading to a rebound in crude oil prices. Brent crude is up 3.4% to $100.61 per barrel, and WTI crude is 3.2% higher at $93.25 a barrel. The ongoing tensions are a primary driver of current market uncertainty.

items.headline: Technology Sector Faces Headwinds from AI Memory Breakthrough
items.summary: Despite being a strong performer recently, the technology sector, particularly memory manufacturers like SK Hynix and Micron Technology, is experiencing declines today. This follows news of a Google breakthrough in AI memory, raising concerns about future demand for existing memory technologies.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Several companies are scheduled to report earnings today. Lovesac ($LOVE) is holding its Q4 2026 earnings call. Commercial Metals Company ($CMC), Rockwell Medical ($RMTI), Blink Charging Co ($BLNK), and Veritone ($VERI) are also among those expected to release their results. After market close, companies like AAR Corp ($AIR), Braze ($BRZE), Argan ($AGX), and Torrid ($CURV) have earnings pending.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Announces Further Job Cuts
items.summary: Meta Platforms ($META) has reportedly slashed approximately 700 jobs across various teams, including Reality Labs and social media divisions, as the company seeks greater cost control amid its substantial investments in AI infrastructure.

items.headline: San Gabriel Valley Economic Forecast Warns of 'Neutral Gear' Economy
items.summary: A new report for the San Gabriel Valley indicates modest economic growth for 2026 but warns that persistent uncertainty, supply chain bottlenecks, and rising operating costs are hindering momentum and straining small businesses. The report describes the regional economy as 'stuck in neutral,' avoiding recession but constrained by these factors.
